hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: 15th percentile is a real, wide gap from the Top 100 median (1428 HPS vs 435 here) — HPM (2.22, see section 03) is also under half the Top 100 median (5.1), so this reads as a throughput/efficiency gap together, not just one metric. Overheal (72.7%) exceeds even the single worst Top 100 parse on this boss (58.4%), the first sign of a pattern that recurs across most of this raid night (see the raid overview).
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lippies's Flash Heal casts this kill are guid 25233 (real mana cost 400), a genuinely different real rank from the Top 100 benchmark's dominant Flash Heal entry (guid 25235, real mana cost 470) — different guid, not a duplicate row (see WORKFLOW.md gotcha #20/#32). Real spell mix otherwise tracks the benchmark's shape closely (same top 4 spells, similar order); the clearest gap is Power Word: Shield at 0% here vs 5.7% average.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Power Word: Shield's ~0% Top 100 usage on this boss is real but misleading — the ranked ladder almost never casts it (likely because shield absorb doesn't count toward the HPS metric it's sorted by), not because using it is bad play; Lippies's own report cast it 8 times across the raid night. Zero real deaths this kill, so no cooldown/death correlation to check. Consumables and cooldown usage otherwise track the Top 100 norm closely.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Top target (34.8%) is close to the Top 100 average's top target (36.5%), and overall coverage (69.7% vs 70.8%) is within normal variance — targeting shape here matches the norm; the real gap on this kill is throughput and overheal, not target spread.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: 24th percentile, the highest of the raid's first two kills. Overheal (44.7%) still exceeds the sample's single worst parse (40.5%) — active time (97.5%) is genuinely above the Top 100 average (95.3%) though, a real positive on this kill specifically.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The clearest gap on this kill: Circle of Healing is only 11.2% of her healing vs. 71.4% average — the Top 100 sample leans on it far more heavily here than she does, while she compensates with more Flash Heal and Prayer of Mending than the norm. This same Circle of Healing gap recurs on most bosses this raid (see the raid overview).
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: 3 real deaths this kill (Captinspanky, Vajony, Hatchpeppers, all within 97s of each other), and Power Word: Shield was never cast — her one real other-targeted defensive tool went unused across a death-heavy kill. Not a direct causal claim (Shield doesn't prevent every death type), but a real, checkable fact worth naming.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (47.8%) and top target share (12.9%) both track the Top 100 average closely (45.3%/11.6%) — targeting spread here is normal; the real gaps this kill are spell composition and HPM.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: 31st percentile is her best of the raid's first four kills, and active time (99.3%) genuinely beats the Top 100 average (96.4%). But this was also the raid's deadliest kill so far (8 real deaths against a 3862-HPS Top 1 parse), and overheal (47.4%) still exceeds the sample's worst (38.4%).
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The largest single spell-composition gap of the raid so far: Circle of Healing is only 2.7% of her healing here vs. 77.3% average — the Top 100 sample's dominant spell on this boss barely features in her own mix, which she replaces almost entirely with Flash Heal (58.3%) and Renew (19.3%, more than double the norm).
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: This is the one kill this raid where Power Word: Shield sees real, active use — 4 real casts on 4 different raiders (Raezy, Churbert, Hatchpeppers, Banepain), all in the ~33-second window from 4516656ms to 4549360ms. That window comes AFTER 5 of the kill's 8 real deaths (all before 4516656ms) and before the remaining 2 (Destromental at 4581953ms, Churbert again at 4604246ms, over 30s after her last Shield cast) — reads as a real, timing-checkable pattern of reactive shielding following an early death cluster, not a claim that Shield prevented or caused any specific death.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (51.0%) runs about 10 points above the Top 100 average (40.8%) — a real but modest concentration gap, plausibly related to a chaotic, high-death fight where healing gets spread reactively rather than in a steady rotation.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: Her best percentile of the raid so far (33rd), and overheal (27.3%) is right at the Top 100 median for once, not exceeding the sample's worst — a real, genuine improvement on this kill. The one real weak spot is active time (88.7%), well below the 97.3% average, worth checking against her own gameplay for this specific pull.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Same recurring pattern: Circle of Healing at 11.5% vs. 63.1% average — the third consecutive kill where the Top 100 sample's dominant heal is a small share of her own mix, replaced mostly by more Flash Heal spam (67.1% vs 0% for the benchmark's own dominant Flash Heal guid).
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: A genuine standout: Fear Ward cast once, on Coday — the Top 100 sample shows 0% usage on this boss, so this is real, uncommon usage that goes against the norm. The 3 real deaths this kill (Shlicktree, Deepbowlz, Danceswtrees) aren't a fear-related mechanic, so no correlation claim to Fear Ward specifically; nothing else in the cooldown timing lines up with the deaths either.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (49.2%) and top target share (16.1% vs. 16.2%) both match the Top 100 norm almost exactly — targeting is not a factor on this kill.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: Active time (99.1%) is near the top of the Top 100 range, and this was a clean, deathless kill — but HPS (728) is still under half the median (1796), and overheal (55.2%) again exceeds the sample's single worst parse (45.4%).
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing again well below norm (15.8% vs. 58.5%) — the fourth consecutive kill with this gap. Prayer of Mending (20.2%) runs well above its own average (8.2%) here, suggesting it's substituting for some of the missing AoE-heal share rather than the deficit being pure throughput loss.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Power Word: Shield's single cast went to Turbarct, who is also her single largest healing target this kill (see section 04) — a coherent, tank-focused pattern rather than a random cast. Zero deaths this kill, so no cooldown/death timing to check.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: The largest targeting gap of the raid so far: 48.3% of her healing went to one target (Turbarct) vs. the Top 100 average's top target getting just 17.0% — nearly triple the normal concentration. This reads as real, dedicated tank-healing on a fight where Turbarct is clearly the primary damage sink, not a targeting mistake, but it is a genuinely different pattern from the rest of the sample.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: This is the longest kill of the raid (498s) and the first where overheal (50.6%) actually falls within the Top 100 sample's real range (best 15.4% to worst 74.6%), rather than exceeding the worst parse — a real, if modest, improvement over most of the raid. HPS (556) is still well under half the median (1280).
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: On this long fight the Top 100 sample spreads healing more evenly across Circle of Healing, Renew, and its own Flash Heal rank (29.8%/24.4%/22.8%); Lippies instead leans over 60% into Flash Heal (guid 25233) alone. A real, sustained-fight difference in playstyle, not just a spot deviation.
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: 5 real deaths this kill (Purehentai, Destromental, Deepbowlz, Vinnyvoss all within 20s of each other, then Captinspanky ~4.5 minutes later) and Power Word: Shield went uncast across this entire 498-second fight — her one real other-targeted defensive cooldown had zero real usage over the raid's longest kill. Fear Ward is real for 31% of the Top 100 sample on this boss specifically; Lippies didn't cast it here.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (44.7%) and top target share (13.6% vs. 13.3%) both track the Top 100 norm closely — targeting is not a factor on this kill.

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: Her lowest percentile of the raid so far (12th) — HPS (410) is under a third of the Top 100 median (1305), and overheal (67.1%) again exceeds the sample's single worst parse (47.3%) by nearly 20 points, the largest such gap yet.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The largest Circle of Healing gap of the raid: 1.6% here vs. 42.4% average. On this kill she leans into Renew instead (44% vs. 14.6% average, a 3x over-index) — Al'ar's real air/ground phase-swap mechanic involves repeated movement, where a HoT that doesn't require standing in range is a plausible, sensible substitute for a channeled or GCD-heavy AoE heal; stated here as a plausible read of the real numbers, not a confirmed cause.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: HPM (2.39) is the lowest efficiency ratio of the raid relative to its own median so far — well under half. Zero deaths this kill, so no cooldown/death timing to check; Power Word: Shield's single real cast went to Vajony.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (66.9%) runs well above the Top 100 average (49.3%) — a real, notable concentration gap, though top target share itself (21.2% vs. 18.2%) is close to normal, so this is spread across the top 5 broadly rather than one single over-focused target.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: Her raw HPS (1585) is the highest of the raid in absolute terms, but this is also the raid's highest-demand fight (Top 100 median 3670 HPS) — 10th percentile, tied for the lowest of the raid. Overheal (47.2%) is roughly double the sample's single worst parse (23.5%), the largest such gap of the whole night, and this kill saw 6 real deaths, the raid's deadliest.
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: A real, notable strategic difference rather than a deficiency: on this raid-wide-damage fight, Lippies leans heavily on Prayer of Healing (65.3%) where the Top 100 sample leans on Circle of Healing instead (81.8%) — both are AoE heals well-suited to this encounter's damage profile, just a different real spell choice, not a missing one.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: The only kill this raid without a food buff active at pull start, a real, specific gap against a 97% Top 100 norm. 6 real deaths this kill (Banepain, Raezy, Churbert, Purehentai simultaneously, Sackajaweeba, Héz), and Power Word: Shield went uncast again — her self-only cooldowns (Shadowfiend, Chakra, 2x Blessing of Life) all fired, but the one tool that could have helped someone else never did.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (67.9%) is nearly double the Top 100 average (37.5%) — a real, substantial spread-healing pattern, consistent with the Prayer of Healing/Circle of Healing-heavy AoE playstyle already seen in section 02 on this raid-wide-damage fight. Her own name appears as her #2 real target, unsurprising on a fight with heavy raid damage.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: Tied for the raid's lowest percentile (10th). Overheal (71.5%) again exceeds the sample's worst parse (58.5%), and active time (93.0%) is a real few points below the median (96.2%) — a genuine gap on this specific kill, more pronounced than most others this raid.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The closest spell-composition match to the Top 100 norm of any kill this raid: Flash Heal + Circle of Healing dominate both her mix (45.7%/35.4%) and the benchmark's (42.8% on its own Flash Heal rank + 40.9% Circle of Healing), in similar proportions. The composition isn't the story on this kill — active time and overheal are.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Real, active use of both other-targeted cooldowns this kill: Power Word: Shield twice (Paladanwood, Destromental) and Fear Ward once (Paladanwood) — Fear Ward is rare in the Top 100 sample here too (only 8% used it), so this is one of the rarer, more proactive kills for utility usage even though HPM (2.31) is her lowest of the raid.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (48.9% vs. 49.0%) essentially matches the Top 100 norm exactly — targeting is a non-factor here, same as spell composition.

kaelthas.SCORECARD_FINDING: Her lowest percentile of the entire raid (7th), on the night's second-longest kill (476s). Active time (88.5%) is below even this fight's own lower sample average (91.4%) — Kael'thas's real forced-movement phases pull every parse's active time down some, but hers still trails. Overheal (66.6%) again exceeds the sample's worst (52.3%).
kaelthas.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Circle of Healing again below norm (17% vs. 43.8%) — the seventh kill this raid with this pattern. Otherwise her mix (Renew, Prayer of Mending, Prayer of Healing all close to their own averages) tracks the benchmark reasonably well outside of the Flash Heal/Circle of Healing split already seen throughout the night.
kaelthas.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Kael'thas is the one fight all raid where Fear Ward is genuinely expected — 85% of the Top 100 sample casts it here, a huge jump from every other boss (0-31% elsewhere), and Lippies matched that real norm with her own cast on Paladanwood. 4 real deaths this kill (Banepain, Sackajaweeba, Deepbowlz, Vinnyvoss — the last two simultaneous), all occurring well after her early Fear Ward cast, so no timing correlation to claim there; Power Word: Shield went uncast again across this 476-second fight.
kaelthas.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (43.0% vs. 43.2%) and top target share (14.2% vs. 13.5%) both essentially match the Top 100 norm — the second kill this raid (with Solarian) where targeting tracks the sample almost exactly.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: identical on 18 of 19 real equipment slots across all 10 real combatantinfo snapshots
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: Ring enchants aren't flagged, per this era's self-only Enchanting restriction. Every socketed slot carries a real gem (14 gem entries total across 6 sockets), confirmed identical on all 10 snapshots.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Overheal exceeds the Top 100 sample's single worst real parse on 7 of 10 kills (Hydross, Leotheras, Morogrim, Alar, Void Reaver, Solarian, Kael'thas), and Circle of Healing usage falls well below the Top 100 average on 7 of 10 kills too (Lurker, Leotheras, Karathress, Morogrim, Alar, Void Reaver, Kael'thas — replaced mostly with more Flash Heal and Renew). These are the two most consistent, raid-wide gaps, and each shows up on the individual boss pages below.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: Percentile sits under 20th on 6 of 10 kills (Hydross, Morogrim, Vashj, Alar, Void Reaver/Solarian tied at 10th, Kael'thas the lowest at 7th) — HPS is under half the Top 100 median on every single kill this raid, the most consistent finding of the night. Karathress (33rd) and Leotheras (31st) are the two real bright spots, both still well below 50th. All 10 percentiles are exact report+fight WCL rankings, not fuzzy-matched.

GearCheckItems.Description: Mainhand carries a permanent enchant and an active temporary weapon oil, on all 10 kills
Ranged weapon (wand) was upgraded partway through the raid, after Lady Vashj
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: A real, benign upgrade, not a gearing gap — same wand equipped for the first 6 kills (Hydross through Lady Vashj), a different, newer wand for the last 4 (Al'ar through Kael'thas Sunstrider). Confirmed from real combatantinfo snapshots on both sides of the swap, not assumed.

GearCheckItems.Description: The shirt slot is genuinely filled (not the usual empty cosmetic slot) — no stat impact either way
